def: s.m. Calling, bidding; call, invitation, summons.
s.m. Calling, bidding; call, invitation, summons.

def: s.m. = H بلاهٿ बुलाहट bulāhaṭ, s.f. = bulāwā, q.v.
s.f. (In Persia and Arabia) the nightingale; and a certain melodious bird resembling the nightingale; (in India) the fork-tailed shrike, Lanius boulboul
more: s. f. 1. A nightingale. 2. (in India) A shrike. (Lanius boulboul. Lath. ).
subs: bulbul-ćashmī, s.m. lit. 'Bulbul-eyed'; a kind of silken cloth, having a cross-bar border with an eye or dot in each of the diamond squares.
ex:
سارے جہاں سے اچھا ہندوستاں ہَمارا
ہم بلبلیں ہیں اس کی، یہ گلستاں ہمارا
sāre jahā̃ se acchā hindostā̃ hamārā
ham bulbulẽ ha͠i is kī, ye gulistā̃ hamārā

Better than the whole world, is our Hindustan;
We are its nightingales, and it is our flower-garden.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلبل (bulbul), from Arabic بُلْبُل (bulbul).

def: s.m. Bubble; any frail thing ( = budbudā).
more: n. m. S. बुद्बुद, Pālī. bubbulam . 1. A bubble. 2. A frail thing. Kyā bharosā hai ziṅdagānī kā, Ādmī bulbulā hai pānī kā .

def: direct plural of بُلْبُل (bulbul, “nightingale”)
oblique plural of بلبل (“nightingale”)
vocative plural of بُلْبُل (bulbul, “nightingale”)
s.m. pl. of balad, q.v.
Buleda (a city in Kech district, Balochistan, Pakistan)
wety: Named after the Buledi tribe.

def: (بو Endowed with, and فضول Excess) adj. Excessive talker, prodigal.

Bulgarian
Bulgaria (a country in Southeastern Europe)
wety: From Arabic بُلْغَارِيَة (bulḡāriya). Compare Pashto بلغاريه (bulǧāryá).

def: (بو Endowed with, and هوس Desire) adj. 1. Desirous. 2. Whimsical, capricious, a wavering fickle (person), a block head.
s. f. Caprice, capriciousness.
s.m. Bubble = bulbulā, q.v. s.m. Bubble; any frail thing ( = budbudā).
more: s. m. A bubble. See بلبلا bulbulā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: s.m. (inf. n. of بلغ 'to reach'), = P بلوغت bulūg̠at, s.f. (for A. بلوغة), Maturity, puberty, full age, adolescence, manhood, attaining of a marriageable age.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُلُوغ (bulūġ), from Arabic بُلُوغ (bulūḡ).

def: s.f. (for A. بلوغة), = A بلوغ bulūg̠, s.m. (inf. n. of بلغ 'to reach'), Maturity, puberty, full age, adolescence, manhood, attaining of a marriageable age.
wety: From Arabic بُلُوغ (bulūḡ). Addition of ـت is likely from analogy with other nouns derived from Arabic, such as آخِرَت (āxirat), حِماقَت (himāqat), طَبِیعَت (tabī'at), عِبادَت ('ibādat), نَباتِیات (nabātiyāt) etc. Doublet of بُلُوغ (bulūġ).

def: s. f. Adolescence, puberty.
s.f. The coming of age, adolescence, &c. ( = bulūg̠ai).
s.m. A large prominent eye.
v.t. To cause to call; to cause to be sent for; to send for, summon, &c. ( = bulānā)
more: v. a. Caus. of بُلانا q. v. Jāte haiṅ, yā usko bulwāte haiṅ ham, Dil ko ye kah kah-ke samjhāte haiṅ ham . Goyā. "Now now I go to her or summon her to me," Thus to my heart I speak and bid it quiet be.
(causal of بلانا bulānā ) v. a. To cause to call or to send for.
subs: bulwā-lenā, v.t. To send for, &c. = bulā-bhejnā, &c., q.v.s.v. bulānā.

ety: Pehl. būm; Zend būmi; S. bhūmi
def: s.m. Land, ground, soil; region, country; — the owl.
more: s. m. 1. An owl. 2. Land, ground (Sans. भूमि ).
wety: From Classical Persian بوم (būm, “earth, land”), from Middle Persian 𐭡𐭥𐭬 (būm), from Old Persian 𐏏 (būmiš), from Proto-Iranian *búHmiš, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *bʰúHmiš.

ety: Pehl. bun; Zend buna; S. बुध्न
def: s.f. Ground, foundation, basis, root, stem (cf. bunyād); bottom, end, tip.
s.m. Coffee, before it is roasted and ground (when ground it is called qahwa).
more: n. The quantity of grain given for a day's weeding, from 2½ to 3 seers.

n. m. Coffee berries not ground. 1. Bun khāo . 1. Chew coffee. 2. Weave and live. 2. Kisī ko qalye kā pyālā, kisī ko bun, supārī, hāë, mujhe khālī rakābī! To some a basin of broth, and betelnuts or coffee some; for me, alas, an empty dish!
s. m. Coffee before it is ground. (When ground, it takes the name قهوه).

def: s.m. Foundation, &c. ( = bun); baggage, goods, household furniture (cf. bun-gāh).
more: s. m. Baggage, goods, household furniture, root, foundation.

def: s.m. The tip of the ear, the lobe of the ear.
more: s. m. The lobe of the ear, the tip of the ear.

def: s.m. The woof of lace.
more: n. m. The woof of lace.

def: n. f. Weftage; texture.
more: n. f. 1. Construction; structure; architecture; formation; constitution; manufacture. 2. Workmanship; work; handiwork. 3. Make; frame; form; figure; shape; cut; set; build; stamp; cast; mould; fashion. 4. Show; display; ostentation; parade. 5. Artificialness; affectation; sham; counterfeit. 6. Fabrication; invention; falsehood; perjury; forgery. 7. Art; artifice; acting; gloss; false coloring. 8. Misstatement; misrepresentation; make-believe.

def: s.f. = būṅd, q.v.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Drop: see بوند būnd.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْد (band).

def: s.f. Drop; drop of rain; — blood; semen; — a kind of spotted silk
more: pl. n. f. S. विन्द drop. 1. A drop; a drop of rain. 1. Būṅd kā chūkā, ghaṛe ḍhalkāve . Prov. The drop not poured in time a cask demands. 2. Woh būṅd vilāyat gaī . Prov. That drop has floated down to a far country. (The opportunity has flown). 2. Blood; semen. Jāne kiskī būṅd hai? Slang. 3. A kind of spotted silk. būṅd ṭapaknā , v. n. To trickle; drip; ooze. būṅd churānā , v. n. ( Slang ). To conceive; to be pregnant. Ye lugāī koṭhī kī sach chī hai, har sāl buṅd churātī hai . Slang. This woman's dark chamber is a true one, every year it retains all that is poured into it. būṅd-choṭṭī , n. f. ( Slang ). A woman prone to conceive. būṅd honā , v. n. 1. To appear as a spot (a kite). 2. To be scarcely visible; hence, to disappear.
s. f. A drop. بوندکي بوند būnd kī būnd, Rectified, twice distilled (spirit, &c.).
adj. 1. Good, well, excellent. 2. High, soaring (generally applied to kites).
subs: excellent, fine, grand, superb (the word is used, in familiar phraseology, to serve as the standard of excellence, &c., and corresponds to our phrases 'the pink of,' 'the essence of')
adv. Drop by drop, by drops; every drop of, to the last drop
v.n. To secrete semen, to conceive (a woman)
adj. Rectified, doubly-distilled (spirit, sharāb), very strong (wine)
v.n. To appear like a speck (a kite); to rise to a great height, to be scarcely visible; (hence) to disappear.

def: s.m. Drop, &c. ( = bind; būṅd); ear-ring ( = bulāq).
more: s. m. A point, a dot.

def: s.m. A large drop: — būṅdā-tāi dī s.f. Small and interrupted dropping of rain; drizzle; distillation, filtration.
more: s. m. A large drop. بونداباندي būndā bāndī, Small and interrupted dropping of rain.

def: s.m. A kind of ornament worn on the ears.
more: s. m. A kind of ornament worn on the ears.

def: n. f. A drizzling shower of rain; a drizzle. Rāt bhar būṅdā-bāṅdī rahī .
s.m. Name of a spurious tribe of Rājpūts who give name to the province of Bundelkhanḍ.
s.f. A woman who sells perfumes.
more: n. f. A woman who sells perfumes.

def: s.f. Small drop; small rain, rain-drops; droppings; a kind of sweetmeat like drops; comfits ( = bundī).
more: s. f. 1. Drops of rain. 2. Name of a place.
s. (dakh.) A kind of sweetmeat ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.f. A kind of sweetmeat (like drops); comfit.
more: s. f. A kind of sweetmeat (like drops).

def: s.f. A little drop, dot, spot, speck
more: n. f. H. būṅd . A little drop; dot; spot. bundkī-dār , adj. ( Physics ). Dotted; punctula. bundkī kī chhīṅṭ , n. f. Spotted chintz.

def: s.f. Small drop; spot, speck, dot ( = bundkī, q.v.). s.f. A little drop, dot, spot, speck
s.f. The rear of an army; baggage, equipage of an army.
more: s. f. Baggage, equipage of an army.

def: fundamentalist
wety: From Urdu بنیاد (bnyād, “basis”) + Urdu پرست (“-ist”).

def: fundamental, primary, basic, principal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بنیادی (buniyādī). By surface analysis, بنیاد (buniyād, “foundation, basis”) + ـی (-ī).

def: basically
s. (dakh.) Mouldiness, mould, mustiness ( Harris ).
v.t. See بننا bunnā.
more: Tir. v. a. S. वे, P. bāftan to weave. To weave; knit; intertwine; interlace.
v. a. To weave, to intertwine.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: s.m. Sprig, stalk, stem; the plant of 'gram' or chick-pea (Cicer arietinum) with the green pods on it; the green pods of the chick-pea; chick-pea, gram (local); — adj. Small, diminutive, stumpy; compact and firm (as a pony, &c.; — cf. but)
more: s. m. 1. A kind of pulse when green (Cicer arietinum): see بوٿ būṭ. 2. The plant of بونٿ būnṭ. 3. adj. Little, compact and firm (epithet applied to a pony, and sometimes used substantively). بونٿ پلاو būnṭ-pulāʼo, s. m. A pulāʼo made of بونٿ.
s. (dakh.) A digit, an inch ( Harris ).
subs: s.m. A dish made of green chick-pea and rice.

def: s.m. = būṭā, q.v. Strength, power, ability.
s.f. = būṭī, q.v.; — name of a game among children. s.f. = būṭā, q.v.; flower or sprig of a plant; a drug; a dot or spot
more: s. f. (dakh.) Name of a game among children: see Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. lvi.

def: s.f. Foundation, basis, base, origin, groundwork; resources, power (syn. aṣl)
more: s. f. Foundation, basis, origin. بنيادڐالنا bunyād ḍālnā, To lay the foundation, to found.
subs: (-kī), To lay the foundation (of), to found; to originate, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بنیاد (bunyād).

def: s.f. Foundation, &c. = bunyād, q.v. s.f. Foundation, basis, base, origin, groundwork; resources, power (syn. aṣl)
subs: (-kī), To lay the foundation (of), to found; to originate, &c.
wety: Perhaps from बनिया (baniyā, “merchant”), characterizing their usual clothing.

def: s.f. A term of abuse applied to a woman, e.g. brimstone, strumpet, slut.
s.f. Chaff, husk; sawdust, &c. ( = būrā); rubbish, trash
more: W. Rus. n. f. H. bhus chaff. Chaff; husk; trash; anything bad. Maiṅ ne kyā is meṅ būr milā dī? būr ke laḍḍū , A sweetmeat made of the husk of grain. Būr ke laḍḍū khāë, to pachhtāë, na khāë to pachhtāë! Prov. Sweet balls of husk, eat and you'll repent, dont eat and you 'll repent. (A siren that allures to destruction).
n. f. S. भूरि दक्षणा alms in plenty. Charity or alms given in marriages, etc. Hin. būr bāṅṭnā , v. a. To give alms at a marriage, etc.

adj. Barren (land). s. f. Chaff, husk. بورکالڐو būr kā laḍḍū, A sort of sweetmeat made with the husk of grain. (The confectioners avow the imposition and cry out to passengers, "youʼll repent if you eat it, or regret not tasting it." Hence, great men are sometimes called بورکالڐو when they make a practice of encouraging their dependants to hope, but do nothing for them at last; as, dancing attendance on them and neglecting them are each likely to produce regret. The term also means, any person or thing, which is fair without and foul within, or that promises well but turns out ill; a well looking, but stupid, fellow).
subs: s.m. A sweetmeat (in the form of balls) made of the husk of grain; (fig.) one who holds out false hopes, or deludes or deceives; anything fair without and foul within, or that promises well but turns out ill; a well-looking person, or person of promising appearance, with nothing in him.
wety: Borrowed from English bore.

def: s.f. = bhūr, q.v. s.f. Hole (through which water runs out); well, or gathering pit (dug by the brink of a stream, chiefly for irrigation: syn. bhurkī).
v. n. To be drowned, to drown.
s.m. (a term of abuse), Scoundrel, rascal, villain.

adj. (f. -ī), Bad, evil, ill, wicked, vicious, evil-minded, immoral, unprincipled, vile, base, villainous, mischievous, injurious, calamitous, destructive, baneful; ugly, ill-looking; disagreeable, unpleasant; odious, hateful; — s.m. Evil, ill, harm, injury; evil person
more: n. m. pl. buroṅ , S. विरूप badly formed. 1. A wicked person; a firebrand. 1. Bure, tujh se daṛye, yā terī burāī se? Prov. Shall I fear thee, foe, or fear the evil thou canst do? 2. Bare se deo ḍarāë . Prov. Even the gods fear the wicked. 2. An enemy; antagonist; foe; rival. Ab kyā rahā ki jis se raqīboṅ kā ḍar kareṅ, Ham to buroṅ kī jān ko pahlī hi ro chuke .

adj. Opp. of achchhā good. 1. Unpleasant; disagreeable; bad ( kharāb ). 2. Ugly; ill-looking; ill-favored; repulsive ( bad-shakl ). Kyā burī sūrat hai! 3. Hateful; odious; abominable; horrible; detestable ( ḍarāonā ). 4. Distasteful; sour; bitter ( bad-mazah ). Kyā burī nāraṅgī hai, muṅh bigaṛ gayā . 5. Obnoxious; hurtful; baneful; pernicious; injurious. Burā khel hai . 6. Poisonous ( zahrīlā ); venomous; deadly; destructive. Burā kīṛā hai . 7. Mischievous; naughty; wicked; vicious. Burā ādmī hai . 8. Evil-minded; immoral; unprincipled. 9. Base; mean; vile; shameless ( be-sharm ). 10. Greedy; covetous ( lālchī ); avaricious. 11. Hard; severe; harsh; strict. 12. Unjust; unfair; inequitable. 13. Merciless ( be-rahm ); pitiless; cruel; ruthless. 14. Rugged ( aughaṭ ); rough; dangerous. 15. Unlucky; sad; deplorable; wretched ( kam-bakht ). Burī ghaṛī āī hai . 16. Peevish; sulky; sullen; iras…
adj. Bad, worse. برالگنا burā lagnā, v. n. To be unpleasant. براماننا burā mānnā, v. a. To take amiss or ill or as an affront, to be displeased or affronted. ¶ burrā, s. See جوف jauf.
adj. (dakh.) Old, old man: see بوڙها būṛhā.
subs: s.m. A sect of Brahmans who perform the obsequies of the dead (syn. aćāraj)
v.t. To depreciate, represent as bad or worthless; to detract, defame, disparage
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Middling, tolerable, passable; — bad or evil person; reproach; abusive language; — burā-bhalā kahnā, To reproach, revile, abuse
s.m. Bad state; sad, deplorable, or wretched condition; — burā ḥāl karnā (-kā), To reduce to a bad or wretched condition; to ill-treat; to ruin
s.m. Any act forbidden by law; malpractice, vice; adultery, fornication (syn. bad-fěʻlī)
(-kā), To do wrong, harm, &c. (to), to wrong, harm, injure
(-ko), To speak ill (of), to pronounce or call (one) bad, evil, wicked, &c.; to vilify, abuse
v.t. To take ill or amiss, to be displeased or angry, to be affronted, feel oneself insulted
burā zamāna, s.m. Evil hour, evil time, evil days, hard times
s.f. Bad thing; bad language, abuse; bad conduct
burī gat, s.f. = burā ḥāl, q.v.
s.f.; — bure din, s.m. = burā waqt, q.v.

def: s.m. Powder ( = buknī); saw-dust, filings (particularly of wood); coarse sugar (syn. khāṅṛ); white sugar ( = ćīnī).
more: s. m. 1. A coarse kind of sugar. 2. Sawdust. 3. Powder.
adj. (dakh.) Bad: see برا burā: s. (dakh.) A mat: see بوريا boriyā.

def: s.m. Redeemable mortgage.

n. m. lit . Bad and good. 1. Abusive language ( khoṭī kharī ). 2. Bad man; evil man. burā bhalā, burī bhalī, burā bāolā , adj. Passable; tolerable; so so ( aisā waisā ). burā bhalā kah'nā , v. a. To reproach. See āṛe hāth lenā (2 to 5). burā hāl, yā ahvāl, burī hālat, yā gat , n. 1. Deplorable or bad state; wretched condition; woful or accursed state; excessive pain; agonies; throes. 1. Nakṭā jīve bure havāl . Prov. The noseless (disgraced) lead a miserable life. 2. Ghar meṅ jiske nār chhināl, wā mānas kā burā havāl . Prov. An adultress for a wife, a curse the husband's life. 2. Straitened circumstances; poverty; misery. burā hāl karnā, patlā hāl karnā , v. a. To reduce to a bad condition; maltreat; ruin. Mār mār-kar burā hāl kar dīyā. burā chītnā, chetnā, chīn'nā, yā chāh'nā , v. n. To think ill of. Dīn o dunyā meṅ uskā hove burā, Jo kisī kā koī burā chīte . Wom. Rangin. In both worlds may they wretched be who evil do in others see. burā rāj , n. m. Mal-administration ( bad-intizāmī ). burā samā , n. m. Bad season; a season of scarcity; dearth; drought; famine ( kāl ). burā kām , n. m. 1. Any act forbidden by law, religious or secular. 2. Adultery; illicit intercourse ( bad-felī ). burā karnā , v. a. To do wrong, hurt, etc. burā likkhā kar'nā , v. a. Mah. Wom. To spoil, hurt, etc. Jān Sāhab, rāt ko phūaṛ-pane se oṛh-kar, Kyā burā likkhā kīyā tum ne hamārī shāl kā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Jan Sāhab, you wore so awkwardly last night My shawl, you have indeed just spoilt it quite. burā lagnā , v. n. Opp. of achchhā lagnā . See achchhā na lagnā . burā mān'nā , v. n. 1. To take ill; take amiss. Tulsī, buro na māniye jo gaṅvār kah jāë, Sāvan kī sī nadduā buro bhalo bah jāë . Dohā. O Tulsī, mind not what the ignorant may say, As August streams both good and bad are borne away. 2. To be angry or displeased. Na burā māne to maiṅ noch lūṅ ek muṭṭhī bhar, Begmā, hai terī kiyārī meṅ harā sāg khaṛā . Wom. Inshā. I'd pluck a handful, ma'am, if you I dont offend, Of potherbs fresh which in your garden grow no end. burā waqt, yā zamānā, burī ghaṛī, bure din . 1. Unlucky star; inauspicious moment. 1. Bhagvān burī ghaṛī na lāë . Prov. Saying. May God ne'er bring on any evil days! 2. Bure waqt kā Allāh belī . Prov. In evil times the Lord our sole defence. 3. Aisī burī ghaṛī āī hai ki sone pe hāth ḍāleṅ to miṭṭī ho jāë . 2. Hard times; evil days. burā ho , intj. Perish! blast thee! See āg lage! Tumhārā burā ho! ki koṭhe pe chaṛh-ke Hazāroṅ ko tāko, hazāroṅ ko ghūro . Wom. Zafar. Heaven blast you, fellow, from the house-top standing you Your wanton eyes cast on all women in your view.
to mind; to take offence

s.m. Filings; sawdust; powder (syn. ćūrā).

def: (from برا burā ) s. f. Mischief, badness, evil, wickedness. براٴي پراٿهناياکمرباندهنا burāʼī par uṭhnā yā kamar bāndhnā, To resolve on mischief.
more: adv. By reason of, on account of, for.

def: s.f. Badness, evil, wickedness, vice, depravity, corruptness, vileness; mischievousness, hurtfulness, destructiveness; defect, harm, mischief: dissension; slander, calumny, detraction; evil consequences or results
more: n. f. See اوگن (1, 4, 6). 1. Vileness; depravity; wickedness. Bure kī burāī meṅ, na bhale kī bhalāī meṅ . Prov. No care at all for ills of evil men, No share in aught of goodness in good men. 2. Hurtfulness; mischievousness; destructiveness; bane. 3. Calumny; detraction; slander (بدگوئي). 4. Frailty; error; weakness; infirmity. 5. Evil consequences or results. burāī bhalāī , n. f. 1. Good and evil; damage; mishap. 2. Blame; charge (الزام 1, 2).
subs: s.f. Good and evil; damage, mishap; reproach, censure, blame, accusation, &c. ( = ilzām)
burāʼī-par kamar baṅdhnā, v.n. To be bent on harm or mischief.

def: s.m. Bad man, bad character, villain; mischief-maker, slanderer; enemy.
more: n. m. 1. A bad man; bad character; mischiefmaker; firebrand. 2. A backbiter; slanderer; detractor. 3. A pilferer ( uchakkā ); a petty thief. 4. A butcher ( qasāī ) (used by herdsmen). Tohe buraīye ke kaṭwāë dūṅ . Rus. (The angry driver to the sluggish ox). 5. A policeman; sepoy. (Gamblers' idiom). Abe, buraīyā āyā, bhāgo! jī burā kar'nā , v. a. To vomit. See ulaṭnā (4).

def: s.m. Borax.

more: Pop. n. m. برق lightning. The horse or Pegasus on which Muhammad is said to have ascended one night from Jerusalem to Heaven, and thence returned to Mecca.

adj. 1. Flashing; shining; brilliant. 2. Swift as the wind; fleet; winged. Ghoṛā kyā hai, burrāq hai . 3. Clever; expert; smart; quick; intelligent. 4. White as snow, crystal, marble, silver, or milk. Kyā burrāq kapṛe dhoë haiṅ .
n. prop. The ass on which Muhammad is said to have passed from Jerusalem to heaven.

def: s.m. = burrās, q.v. s.m. The tree Rhododendron arboreum (common on the outer ranges of the Himalaya from the Indus to Bhūṭān; also written burrāṅs, būrāṅs.
s.f. = buṛ-bhas, buṛh-bhas, q.v.
more: n. f. H. buṛ=buḍḍhā old, bhas= bhak=bhog carnal enjoyment. The affectation of youth in old age. Muṅh kālā kare kaun, lagī usko hai buṛ-bhas, Sir hiltā hai, par shauq hai missī kī dhaṛī kā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Who fain would blacken her face; yet see this old thing's lickerish bent, Her head with age is shaking, still her teeth she needs must paint.

more: v. n. 1. To murmur; mutter; grumble. 1. Dil meṅ kuchh ho to muṅh se bol uṭho, Kyoṅ gulāmoṅ se buṛbuṛāte ho? Soz. If in your mind you've aught then say it out, Why like a slave go murmuring about? 2. Baṅkkāro āj khūb, chalo maë-kade ko, Zauq, Chhoṛo kahīṅ vazīfah, bahut buṛbuṛā chuke . Zauq. Now to the tavern, Zauq, uproarious revel there, Your prayers cease, you've chattered quite enough, forbear. 2. To prate; babble; talk nonsense; rave. Buṛbuṛātī hai tū kyā, subah ko kal, rah to sahī, Haḍḍī, haḍḍī terī karnī hai mujhe chūr, dadā . Wom. Rangiṅ. Yes murmur on, my friend, but just you wait till dawn, I'll break your bones and grind them into powder, spawn.

def: s.m. See s.v. buṛ. s.f. A term of abuse applied to a woman, e.g. brimstone, strumpet, slut.

ety: v.n. — or 3rd pers. sing. pret. — of burdan, 'to carry, &c.
def: s.f. Bearing, carrying; bearing or carrying off; capture, prize, acquisition, gain, profit, haul, catch; pocketings, pickings, perquisites, bribes; stake, wager, bet; (in chess) the state in which an adversary has lost all his pieces, the king alone remaining, the game so won (it is not considered so creditable as 'check-mate')
more: n. f. بُردن to carry. See بالائي اَمد 1. Gain; profit; prize. Baṛī burd nikal gaī . 2. Perquisites; pickings; douceur; bribes. 3. Bet ( hoṛ ); wager. Āo pāṅch pāṅch rupaë kī burd sahī . Slang. 4. (In chess). The king, the only piece remaining on one side of the board. burd de'nā , v. n. To lose; miss; forfeit. Woh chāl tum chale ki dīyā burd sārā ghar, Ab bāqī maiṅ hūṅ, dāoṅ pe mujh ko lagāīye . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Your way of life hath ruin wrought, of all bereft, Now stake your wife, from all the wreck she only left. burd mȧrnā , v. a. 1. To gain a victory; win the game. 2. To have a windfall, perquisite, douceur or bribe.
s. f. A term used at chess when the king only, on one side, remains on the board; (hence, also) an opportunity of making some acquisition.
(from بردن) s. A bearing, a carrying.
subs: adj. lit. 'Bearing a burden'; forbearing, tolerant, longsuffering, patient, gentle, mild, meek; — burd-bārī, s.f. Bearing of a burden; forbearance, toleration, longsuffering, patience, meekness, gentleness
v.t. To lose, miss, forfeit; to waste, squander
v.n. To gain a victory, win the game (at chess); to make an acquisition; make a haul, have a windfall, &c.

def: s.f. = buṛhāpā, q.v. s.m. Old age, senility.
Rus. n. f. A woman. Kar-kar pīt ḍigar gayo rasyā, burhal aṅg aṅg bhabūt lagāë . Song. A gay and wanton youth did take her heart and go away, the woman mourns him ashes-clad!

s.m. Old age, senility, senile decrepitude (syn. buṛhāpā).
s.f. = buṛhaʼǒtā, q.v.

n. m. H. būṛhā old man. Old age; the decline of life. 1. Kyā qahr hai, yāro, jise ā jāë buṛhāpā! Aur aish javānī ke taīṅ khāë buṛhāpā! Ishrat ko milā khāk meṅ, gam lāë buṛhāpā! Har kām ko, har bāt ko tarsāë buṛhāpā! Sab chīz ko hotā hai burā, hāë buṛhāpā! Āshiq ko to Allah na dikhlāë buṛhāpā! On old age. Nazīr. What vengeance falls on him to whom doth come old age! Swallowed are youthful pleasures all by dire old age! All keen delights cast in the dust, grief-bought old age! Vain thirstings after joys which come not to old age! All things, all hearts have felt thy blast, alas! old age! On lovers, God, ne'er bring that withered thing, old age! 2. Hāë buṛhāpe! Alas, old age! "Hāë buṛhāpe!"—"Javānī meṅ kyā patthar paṛte the?" "Alas, I am old now!"—"Well, but what great feat did I execute in my youth!" (The first portion is uttered in the supposed hearing of listeners. On turning round, the vain old man sees no body by. His conscience asserts itself, and he owns to himself the humiliating fact mentioned in the last portion of his speech.
s.m. Old age, senility.
wety: Related to بوڑھا (būṛhā, “old man”). Compare Punjabi بُڑھاپا (buṛā̀pā) and Saraiki بُݙھیپا (buḏhepā).

s.m. See s.v. A. bū. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

To-day betrothed, to-morrow is the wedding day, You break your leg—your marriage you must now delay. 2. Biyāh nahīṅ kiyā to kyā, barāteṅ to dekhī haiṅ . Prov. If I never married, what then? Have n't I seen many a wedding? (If it has n't happened to me, have I not seen it happen to others?) byāh rachānā , v. a. To celebrate a marriage. Lālā jī ne biyāh rachāyā laiṅhgā bech lugāī kā, Māshā bhar kī chār kachaurī, khurmā māshe ḍhāī kā, Ghar meṅ roveṅ bahan, bhānjī, bāhar rove nāī kā , Play. Chaubolā . Did ever you see such a wedding queer, As the Lalā's who sold his wife's skirt for a stiver? A half drachm just of dāl cakes four, Of sweetmeats two and a half, no more, Sisters and nieces crying at home, The barber without with wailing o'erc…

def: v.n. To bring forth, give birth (to, — applied to animals), to calve, foal, farrow, litter, pup, yean.
more: v. n. To be delivered of young (applied to animals only), to calve, foal, farrow, litter, pup, yean.

def: s.m. Sarcasm; covert but intelligible expression of suspicion or contempt.
s.m. = byān, q.v. s.m. Bringing forth, act of parturition, birth; — adv. At a birth (usually with ek).
more: Bhoj. n. m. Birth; giving birth to (animals). Sūrī kā sā biyāṅt .

def: s.m. The space between the tips of the fingers of either hand when the arms are extended, a fathom.
more: n. m. A measure equal to the length of both arms extended from end to end, including the chest.

def: s.m. Business, affair, traffic, &c. = baipār, q.v. s.m. Traffic, trade, merchandise; business, labour
s.m. Man of business in general; dealer, trader ( = baipārī, q.v.). s.m. Trader, merchant.
v.t. To reach through, spread through, pervade, penetrate, fill, fill up, occupy; to act, operate, work, produce effect; to affect, to prevail.
more: v. n. S. वि + आप to occupy the mind. 1. To occupy; dwell in; pervade; fill. 1. Byāp rahyo woh ghaṭ ghaṭ māhīṅ . Bhajan. God dwelleth in every mind. 2. Sāre badan meṅ garmī biyāp gaī . 2. To be affected, influenced. 3. To come in one's mind; occur to or strike one. Yeh sansā mohe nis din byāpe, koī na kah samjhāë, Dhāgā ṭūṭā, gagan binas gayā, shabdā kahāṅ samāë? Kabīr. All night and day I seek to know, but none can light provide, The breath once out, the body dead, where doth the soul abide?
v. a. To pervade, occupy, effect, operate, to work, to act, affect.

def: s.f. = biyārī, q.v.
s.f. Supper ( = byālū).
more: s. f. Supper. See بيالو byālū.

def: adj. Ineffectual, meaningless, unmeaning, vain, useless, unprofitable.
adj. Vain.
s.m. Name of a celebrated saint and author, the supposed original compiler of the Vedas and Purāṇas; also the founder of the Vedanta philosophy.
more: s. m. A celebrated saint and author, the supposed original compiler of the Vedas and Purāṇas; also, the founder of the Vedānta philosophy.

def: adj. Eighty-two.
part. Pained, distressed; alarmed.

n. f. A married woman, in contradistinction to a kept woman (Hin. karī-huī ; Mah. nikāhtā ). Nikāhī, na biyāhī, munḍo bahū kahāṅ se āī . Prov. Nor wedded wife, nor concubine; this shaven head no daughter-in-law of mine.
part. Passed, elapsed, &c. ( = bītā; see bītnā).
adj. Explicitly, distinctly.
s.m. = byāhlā, q.v. s.m. Marriage song of a god.
more: n. m. A marriage song of a god.

def: s.f. = byāhlī, q.v. s.f. Newly-married woman, bride.
n. f. A newly married woman; a bride.
s.m. Disappearance, loss, destruction; expenditure, disbursement, expense, consumption, use; squandering, wasting.
s.m. Exercising, practising; exertion, labour.
more: s. m. Exertion, labour, exercising, practising.

def: s.m. Expenditure, &c. ( = byay, q.v.). s.m. Disappearance, loss, destruction; expenditure, disbursement, expense, consumption, use; squandering, wasting.

s.m. Interest (on money), &c. = byāj, q.v. s.m. Deceit, fraud, cunning, craft; disguise (either of purpose or of person); semblance, pretext, pretence; contrivance, means; surplus, deduction, discount, interest (on money), usury
subs: s.m. A comprehensive term for the various items of the business of a shroff or banker
s.m. Interest upon interest, compound interest
byāj-ḵẖora, s.m. One who takes interest, an usurer

def: s.m. Loan.
s.m. Profession, calling, trade, business; dealing, traffic, trade, transaction; money-lending; negotiation, correspondence, intercourse; practice, usage, custom, conduct, practical life
more: s. m. Profession, calling, trade, negociation, transaction, practice, custom.
subs: v.n. To do or transact business, to trade; to set up business; to negotiate (with), deal or treat (with); to bargain.

def: s.m. Trader, money-lender; creditor.
more: s. m. A creditor, a lender.

def: adj. & s.m. Mercantile, commercial; wonted, customary, usual; — trader, merchant; party engaged in any business or affair; money-lender.
s.m. Trader, money-lender; creditor.
more: s. m. A creditor, a lender.

def: s.m. An instrument with which leather is scraped and cleaned
more: n. m. A tool with which leather is scraped and cleaned. Is par byoṅgā nahīṅ phirā . This hide has not been tanned yet. (Said of a spoilt child, etc.)
s. m. The instrument with which leather is scraped and cleaned. بيونگاپهرانـﮩين byongā phirā nahīṅ, His hide has not been curried yet (said of a spoiled child, &c.).
subs: (-par), To be curried (leather); — is-par byoṅgā nahīṅ phirā, His hide has not been curried yet (said cf a spoiled child, &c.).

def: s.f. Dim. of byoṅgā, q.v.
s.m. The act of cutting out clothes; out, fit, shape, fashion; plan, scheme, contrivance; suitable or proper time or season, opportunity; agreement, concurrence, union; economy, frugality.
more: n. m. S. ब्योंत separation, वि cloth. 1. Measuring one for a suit of clothes. Pahle bauṅt kar lo, phir phāṛnā . 2. Cut; fit; shape; fashion. Aṅgarkhe kā bauṅt burā hai . 3. Plan; scheme; contrivance. 4. Agreement; accord; concurrence; union. Bauṅt khāëgā to deṅge . 5. Economy; frugality; husbandry; good housewifery. 6. Suitable or proper time or season; opportunity. Bauṅt banā to āëṅge .
s. m. Shape, fashion, the act of cutting out clothes.

def: s.m. = byoṅt, q.v. s.m. The act of cutting out clothes; out, fit, shape, fashion; plan, scheme, contrivance; suitable or proper time or season, opportunity; agreement, concurrence, union; economy, frugality.
v.t. = byoṅtnā, q.v. v.t. To cut out or shape clothes; to measure for, or to fit, clothes; — to cut up, hack, kill, slay.
v.t. To cut out or shape clothes; to measure for, or to fit, clothes; — to cut up, hack, kill, slay.
more: v. a. 1. To measure (for a coat, etc.); to cut or fit clothes. 2. To kill; slay; despatch; do for one.
v. a. To cut or shape clothes.

def: v. a. Caus. of بيونتنا q. v.
s.m. Traffic, trade, business, labour ( = baipār; byohār).
more: n. m. S. ब्यापार from पृ to trade. Trade (بيوﮩـار 1.).

def: s.m. Dealer, trader, merchant ( = baipārī).
more: n. m. S. ब्यापारी. 1. A dealer; merchant; trader; a travelling trader or pedlar. 2. A butcher who trades in sheep and cattle.
s. m. A merchant. See بيپاري baipārī.

ety: S. व्यवहारः, rt. हृ with वि and अव
def: s.m. Difference, distinction; account, explanation, history, circumstantial or detailed account ( = tafṣīl); news, tidings, intelligence; day-book
more: n. m. S. व्याहृ to utter. See برنن 1. News; tidings; intelligence. 2. A detailed account; a day book. byo'rā, karnā, yā denā , v. a. To inform; intimate. byore-wār , adj. Circumstantial; detailed ( bil tafsīl ); distinct; explicit. byore-wār , adv. In detail; explicitly; distinctly (بالتفصيل).
s. m. 1. Difference, distinction. 2. Account, explanation, history, detail of circumstances.
subs: byorā karnā (-kā), To relate, describe; to inform, intimate
beʼore-wār, adj. & adv. Circumstantial, detailed, explicit, distinct, clear, plain; — circumstantially, in detail, explicitly, &c. (syn. tafṣīl-wār; biʼt-tafṣīl).

def: s.m. = byoṅt, q.v. s.m. The act of cutting out clothes; out, fit, shape, fashion; plan, scheme, contrivance; suitable or proper time or season, opportunity; agreement, concurrence, union; economy, frugality.
